title: dryopteris aurita
intro: Dryopteris aurita, also known as the auriculated wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dryopteris aurita include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the plant as a tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and proper uses.
